data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T204710-824bdd56/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T204710-824bdd56/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T204710-824bdd56/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T204710-824bdd56/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T204710-824bdd56/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-c113a116">
    <title>bead/store: unify Ready definition - drop redundant Status==Open filter</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Two places answer "is this bead ready?" and they disagree. The lifecycle
  module (lifecycle.go) classifies a bead's queue bucket from facts; the
  store.go Ready() helper then re-filters by raw status, producing a different
  answer than the lifecycle classifier when an in_progress bead has a stale
  claim.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:155-208 EvaluateLifecycleQueue routes
    StatusInProgress at line 190-194 through evaluateOpenLifecycleQueue when
    !ClaimFresh, which can produce LifecycleBucketReady at line 232.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1754-1766 readyFiltered then iterates the
    classifier output and applies a second filter at line 1763:
        if entry.Status == StatusOpen { ready = append(...) }
    This filter is gated by !executionOnly, so external readers
    (ddx bead ready non-executionOnly path) see a different result than
    queue-execution callers (executionOnly path).
  - Net effect: a stale-claimed in_progress bead with deps satisfied is
    "ready" per EvaluateLifecycleQueue, "not ready" per store.Ready() in
    operator-facing mode, and "ready" per store.Ready() in execution mode.
    Three answers; one question.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Decide the canonical answer: per TD-031 §6, claim metadata is not status,
    and a stale-claimed in_progress bead is recoverable through the heartbeat
    TTL mechanism. The lifecycle module's classifier already encodes this.
    Therefore the second filter at store.go:1763 is redundant and incorrect.
  - Edit cli/internal/bead/store.go:1754-1766: remove the
    "if entry.Status == StatusOpen" branch; both executionOnly and
    operator-facing paths consume LifecycleBucketReady directly.
  - Add regression test TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady in
    cli/internal/bead/store_test.go (or extend an existing nearby test) that:
      a. Creates a bead, claims it (open -> in_progress).
      b. Manually expires the heartbeat (set claimed-at + heartbeat TTL).
      c. Asserts Ready() returns the bead in operator-facing mode (not just
         the executionOnly path).
  - Verify no other call sites of readyFiltered or Ready depend on the
    removed filter; grep for s.Ready, store.Ready, .Ready( across cli/.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing EvaluateLifecycleQueue or the lifecycle bucket enumeration.
  - Adding new claim-recovery semantics.
  - Migrating callers between executionOnly and non-executionOnly paths.

INTERSECTIONS
  - ddx-ca1c66ee (epic parent).
  - No deps on B1/B2; can run in parallel with the TD-031 amendment.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go readyFiltered no longer applies a Status==StatusOpen filter; both executionOnly and operator-facing paths return entries whose Decision.Bucket==LifecycleBucketReady.
2. TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go asserts a stale-claimed in_progress bead with satisfied deps appears in Ready() output.
3. No other test in cli/internal/bead/... regresses (existing Ready/Blocked/queue tests stay green).
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:bead, area:lifecycle, kind:fix, spec:TD-031</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ca1c66ee" spec-id="TD-031" base-rev="6b96701e64764c6fbd5345360bb5757d9e42feb8" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T204710-824bdd56"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="TD-031" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md">Technical Design: Bead State Machine and Naming-Role Decisions</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All ACs were already satisfied by commit `cc5e983ba` before this execution began:

- **AC1**: `readyFiltered` (store.go:1813-1830) has no `Status==StatusOpen` filter — both paths consume `LifecycleBucketReady` directly.
- **AC2**: `TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady` (store_test.go:759) exists and passes.
- **AC3/AC4**: Full suite `./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` — all green.

Written `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command` to close the bead cleanly.
